news_items.headline: 'Bryce is for real': Elder continues hot start with 9 K's in Seattle
Padres slug their way to 10-5 win over SF Giants
news_items.summary: Logan Webb got rocked and Song Sung-Mun starred in his first big-league game as the Padres won, 10-5

news_items.headline: Valdez tossed as Hinch questions LHP's actions
news_items.summary: Tigers manager A.J. Hinch questioned the actions of his own pitcher, Framber Valdez, after the left-hander hit Trevor Story with a pitch following back-to-back Boston Red Sox home runs Tuesday night in Detroit.

news_items.headline: Astros' Correa (ankle) scratched from lineup
news_items.summary: Astros shortstop Carlos Correa was scratched from Tuesday night's lineup against the Los Angeles Dodgers after hurting his left ankle in the batting cage before the game.

news_items.headline: Reds closer Pagan (hamstring) carted off, IL-bound...
news_items.summary: Reds closer Emilio Pagan will be put on the injured list after being carted off in the ninth inning Tuesday because of a left hamstring injury.

news_items.headline: Pirates' Devenski, Kelly both suspended, fined
news_items.summary: Pirates reliever Chris Devenski and manager Don Kelly have both been suspended and fined after Devenski intentionally threw at Reds rookie Sal Stewart during Saturday's game.

news_items.headline: Red Sox OF Anthony has sprained wrist, dodges IL
news_items.summary: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony, sent back to Boston to see a hand specialist on Monday, was diagnosed with a sprained right wrist, but he will not be placed on the injured list yet, interim manager Chad Tracy told reporters on Tuesday in Detroit.

news_items.headline: Yankees to honor John Sterling with jersey patch
news_items.summary: The Yankees announced Tuesday they will wear a patch on their jersey sleeves as a tribute to John Sterling, the team's longtime radio voice who died Monday at age 87.

news_items.headline: Rodon, Cole give up 6 runs each in rehab starts
news_items.summary: New York Yankees pitcher Carlos Rodón allowed six runs, five of them earned, and seven hits in 6⅓ innings during his third and likely final minor league rehabilitation start for Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre on Tuesday.

news_items.headline: Twins' Ryan (elbow) could make his next start
news_items.summary: Twins right-hander Joe Ryan, who left Sunday's win because of soreness in his elbow, does not appear to have any structural damage in his arm.

news_items.headline: Journey to .500 on hold as Angels defeat White Sox, 4-3
news_items.summary: Mike Trout and Co. took Fedde deep three times while Chicago left 11 runners on base in Tuesday’s loss

news_items.headline: George Kirby grounds Braves, Mariners strikeout 16 times in 3-2 loss
news_items.summary: Kirby features sweeper in another strong outing

news_items.headline: Happy .500 de Mayo! Dbacks 9, Pirates 0
news_items.summary: On the same day that manager Torey Lovullo called the entire starting rotation into his office to tell them they needed to pick up the pace, Dbacks starter Eduardo Rodriguez did just that and then some. ERod put this team on his back tonight shutting down the Pirates offense taking some of the pressure off […]

news_items.headline: Astros shortstop Carlos Correa reportedly out indefinitely with ankle injury suffered in warmups before win over Dodgers
news_items.summary: Carlos Correa was taking swings in a batting cage before Tuesday's game when he injured his ankle.

news_items.summary: Nick Morabito and Ryan Clifford each recorded two hits on Tuesday as they continued their good starts at the plate this year at Triple-A Syracuse.

news_items.headline: Matt Olson’s 9th inning homer sends Braves to 3-2 win over Mariners
news_items.summary: A night after letting another win slip through their fingers, the Atlanta Braves battled back to even the series in Seattle against the Mariners with a 3-2 victory on Tuesday. In the top of the ninth, Matt Olson launched a solo homer off Mariners closer Andrés Munoz to give the Braves a 3-2 lead. Olson […]
